docs.title: PresentationSpec
docs.text: A Go-first specification library for creating data-driven presentations.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
PresentationSpec provides a format-agnostic intermediate representation (IR) for presentations, enabling:

  • Go-first design: Go types are the source of truth, JSON Schema is generated automatically
  • Multiple output formats: HTML, PDF, and more
  • Rich widget library: 12 widget types optimized for dashboards and executive presentations
  • Data integration: Connect to JSON files, CSV, and REST APIs
  • Live development: Dev server with hot reload for rapid iteration

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "metadata": {
    "title": "Q1 Results",
    "author": "Product Team"
  },
  "slides": [
    {
      "id": "title",
      "type": "title",
      "title": "Q1 2025 Results"
    },
    {
      "id": "metrics",
      "type": "dashboard",
      "title": "Key Metrics",
      "widgets": [
        {
          "id": "revenue",
          "type": "metric_card",
          "title": "Revenue",
          "value": "1.2M",
          "unit": "$",
          "status": "good",
          "trend": "up",
          "trendValue": "+15%"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• :material-code-braces:{ .lg .middle } Go-First Design

    Define presentations in Go types. JSON Schema is generated automatically for validation and editor support.

  • :material-file-pdf-box:{ .lg .middle } Multiple Formats

    Export to HTML for web viewing, PDF for printing, with more formats planned.

  • :material-chart-box:{ .lg .middle } Rich Widgets

    12 widget types including metric cards, charts, tables, risk lists, and more.

  • :material-database:{ .lg .middle } Data Sources

    Load data from JSON files, CSV, REST APIs with authentication support.

  • :material-refresh:{ .lg .middle } Live Reload

    Development server with automatic rebuild on file changes.

  • :material-presentation:{ .lg .middle } Speaker Notes

    Presenter view with timer, notes, and slide previews.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
go install github.com/grokify/presentation-spec/cmd/render@latest
go install github.com/grokify/presentation-spec/cmd/genschema@latest

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
1. Create a presentation spec in JSON
  2. Render to HTML: render presentation.json -o output/
  3. Open output/index.html in a browser

See the Quick Start Guide for a complete tutorial.

docs.title: Widget Types
docs.text: Widget Description metric_card KPI metrics with trends and sparklines chart Bar, line, pie, area charts via Chart.js table Data tables with configurable columns risk_list Risk items with severity and mitigation decision_list Decisions requiring action checklist Task lists with completion status content_block Markdown/HTML content diagram Mermaid, PlantUML, Graphviz diagrams callout Info, warning, error, success callouts quote Quotations with attribution image Images with captions code Code blocks with syntax highlighting

docs.title: Basic Example
docs.text: A minimal presentation to get started.

docs.title: hello-world.json
docs.text:
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "metadata": {
    "title": "Hello World",
    "author": "Your Name",
    "date": "2025-01-15"
  },
  "theme": {
    "name": "default",
    "aspectRatio": "16:9"
  },
  "slides": [
    {
      "id": "title",
      "type": "title",
      "title": "Hello, PresentationSpec!",
      "widgets": [
        {
          "id": "subtitle",
          "type": "content_block",
          "content": "A simple presentation"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "content",
      "type": "content",
      "title": "Key Points",
      "widgets": [
        {
          "id": "points",
          "type": "content_block",
          "content": "## What is PresentationSpec?\n\n- JSON-based presentation format\n- Go-first design with generated schema\n- Multiple output formats (HTML, PDF, PPTX)",
          "format": "markdown"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "summary",
      "type": "summary",
      "title": "Thank You",
      "keyMessage": "Get started at github.com/grokify/presentation-spec"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Rendering
docs.text:
Generate HTML:

presspec render hello-world.json --output ./output

Generate PDF:

presspec render hello-world.json --output ./output --format pdf

docs.title: Rendering
docs.text:
# HTML output
presspec render launch-readiness.json --output ./output

# With speaker notes
presspec render launch-readiness.json --output ./output --speaker-notes

# PDF output
presspec render launch-readiness.json --output ./output --format pdf

# Watch mode for development
presspec serve launch-readiness.json --port 8080

docs.title: Features Demonstrated
docs.text:
• Metadata - Title, description, author, tags
  • Theme - Brand colors, logo, typography
  • Narrative - Storyline and sections
  • Dashboard layout - KPI row with content grid
  • Metric cards - Values, trends, targets, sparklines
  • Charts - Line chart with multiple series
  • Checklist - Task completion status
  • Risk list - Severity, mitigation, owner
  • Decision list - Deadlines and owners
  • Table - Structured data display
  • Speaker notes - Presenter guidance

docs.title: render
docs.text: The main command for rendering PresentationSpec files.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
render [spec.json] [flags]
render [command]

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Command Description serve Start a dev server with live reload help Help about any command completion Generate shell completion scripts

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Short Default Description --output -o <name>-output/ Output directory (HTML) or file (PDF) --file -f Output single HTML file (no assets) --format html Output format: html, pdf --validate false Validate spec before rendering --validate-only false Validate only, don't render --watch -w false Watch for changes and auto-rebuild --speaker-notes false Generate speaker notes view --page-size Letter PDF page size: Letter, A4, Legal, Tabloid --landscape true PDF landscape orientation --help -h Show help

docs.title: Render to HTML
docs.text:
# Default output directory (presentation-output/)
render presentation.json

# Custom output directory
render presentation.json -o dist/

# Single HTML file (no assets)
render presentation.json -f presentation.html

docs.title: Render to PDF
docs.text:
# Default output (presentation.pdf)
render presentation.json --format pdf

# Custom output path
render presentation.json --format pdf -o report.pdf

# A4 paper size
render presentation.json --format pdf --page-size A4

# Portrait orientation
render presentation.json --format pdf --landscape=false

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
# Validate before rendering
render presentation.json --validate -o output/

# Validate only (no output)
render presentation.json --validate-only

docs.title: Watch Mode
docs.text:
# Watch for changes
render presentation.json -o output/ --watch

docs.title: Speaker Notes
docs.text:
# Generate with speaker notes view
render presentation.json -o output/ --speaker-notes

docs.title: render serve
docs.text: Start a development server with live reload.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: render serve [spec.json] [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Short Default Description --port -p 8080 Port to listen on --host localhost Host to bind to --output -o <name>-output/ Output directory --no-watch false Disable auto-rebuild --no-browser false Don't open browser on start

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Default (localhost:8080)
render serve presentation.json

# Custom port
render serve presentation.json --port 3000

# Bind to all interfaces
render serve presentation.json --host 0.0.0.0

# Disable auto-rebuild
render serve presentation.json --no-watch

docs.title: genschema
docs.text: Generate JSON Schema from Go types.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: genschema [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Short Default Description --output -o schema/presentation.schema.json Output path

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Default output
genschema

# Custom output path
genschema -o docs/schema.json

docs.title: Shell Completion
docs.text: Generate shell completion scripts for enhanced CLI experience.

docs.title: Bash
docs.text: render completion bash > /etc/bash_completion.d/render

docs.title: Zsh
docs.text: render completion zsh > "${fpath[1]}/_render"

docs.title: Fish
docs.text: render completion fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/render.fish

docs.title: PowerShell
docs.text: render completion powershell | Out-String | Invoke-Expression

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Go 1.21 or later
  • Chrome or Chromium (for PDF export)

docs.title: Install CLI Tools
docs.text:
Install the render command for generating presentations:

go install github.com/grokify/presentation-spec/cmd/render@latest

Install the schema generator (optional, for development):

go install github.com/grokify/presentation-spec/cmd/genschema@latest

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
render --help

You should see:

render converts a PresentationSpec JSON file into a static HTML presentation or PDF.

Output formats:
  - html (default): Static HTML with CSS/JS assets
  - pdf: PDF document via headless Chrome

Usage:
  render [spec.json] [flags]
  render [command]

Available Commands:
  serve       Start a dev server with live reload
  ...

docs.title: Use as a Library
docs.text:
Add PresentationSpec to your Go project:

go get github.com/grokify/presentation-spec

Import the packages you need:

import (
    "github.com/grokify/presentation-spec/spec"
    "github.com/grokify/presentation-spec/templ"
    "github.com/grokify/presentation-spec/schema"
)

brew install --cask google-chrome

wget https://dl.google.com/linux/direct/google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb
sudo dpkg -i google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb

Download from google.com/chrome

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: Create your first presentation in 5 minutes.

docs.title: Step 1: Create a Presentation Spec
docs.text:
Create a file called hello.json:

{
  "version": "1.0",
  "metadata": {
    "title": "Hello PresentationSpec",
    "description": "My first presentation",
    "author": "Your Name"
  },
  "theme": {
    "name": "corporate",
    "aspectRatio": "16:9"
  },
  "slides": [
    {
      "id": "title",
      "type": "title",
      "title": "Hello PresentationSpec",
      "widgets": [
        {
          "id": "subtitle",
          "type": "content_block",
          "content": "A Go-first presentation library"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "features",
      "type": "content",
      "title": "Features",
      "keyMessage": "Everything you need for data-driven presentations",
      "widgets": [
        {
          "id": "feature-list",
          "type": "checklist",
          "items": [
            {"text": "Go-first design", "checked": true},
            {"text": "Multiple output formats", "checked": true},
            {"text": "Rich widget library", "checked": true},
            {"text": "Data integration", "checked": true}
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "metrics",
      "type": "dashboard",
      "title": "Sample Dashboard",
      "widgets": [
        {
          "id": "metric1",
          "type": "metric_card",
          "title": "Users",
          "value": "10K",
          "status": "good",
          "trend": "up",
          "trendValue": "+25%"
        },
        {
          "id": "metric2",
          "type": "metric_card",
          "title": "Revenue",
          "value": "1.2M",
          "unit": "$",
          "status": "good"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Step 2: Render to HTML
docs.text:
render hello.json -o hello-output/

This creates:

hello-output/
├── index.html
└── assets/
    ├── style.css
    └── navigation.js

docs.title: Step 3: View the Presentation
docs.text:
Open hello-output/index.html in a browser.

Use arrow keys or click the navigation buttons to move between slides.

docs.title: Step 4: Use Watch Mode
docs.text:
For rapid iteration, use watch mode:

render hello.json -o hello-output/ --watch

Edit hello.json and the output rebuilds automatically.

docs.title: Step 5: Start the Dev Server
docs.text:
For the best development experience, use the dev server:

render serve hello.json

This starts a server at http://localhost:8080 with live reload.

docs.title: Step 6: Export to PDF
docs.text: render hello.json --format pdf -o hello.pdf

docs.title: Step 7: Add Speaker Notes
docs.text:
Add speaker notes and generate the presenter view:

{
  "id": "metrics",
  "type": "dashboard",
  "title": "Sample Dashboard",
  "speakerNotes": "Emphasize the 25% user growth this quarter.",
  "widgets": [...]
}

Generate with speaker notes view:

render hello.json -o hello-output/ --speaker-notes

Open hello-output/speaker.html for the presenter view with timer and notes.

docs.title: Building Dashboards
docs.text: This guide covers creating data-rich dashboard presentations.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Dashboards combine multiple widgets to display KPIs, charts, and status information. PresentationSpec provides specialized layouts and widgets for dashboard-style slides.

docs.title: Dashboard Layout
docs.text:
Use the kpi_row_content_grid template for typical dashboards:

{
  "id": "metrics-dashboard",
  "type": "dashboard",
  "title": "Q1 Metrics",
  "layout": {
    "template": "kpi_row_content_grid",
    "regions": [
      {"id": "kpi-row", "role": "kpi"},
      {"id": "main", "role": "content", "columns": 3},
      {"id": "sidebar", "role": "sidebar", "columns": 1}
    ]
  }
}

This creates:

┌─────┬─────┬─────┬─────┐
│ KPI │ KPI │ KPI │ KPI │
├─────┴─────┴─────┼─────┤
│                 │     │
│     Content     │Side │
│                 │     │
└─────────────────┴─────┘

docs.title: KPI Metrics Row
docs.text:
Add metric cards to the KPI region:

{
  "widgets": [
    {
      "id": "revenue",
      "type": "metric_card",
      "regionId": "kpi-row",
      "title": "Revenue",
      "value": "1.2M",
      "unit": "$",
      "status": "good",
      "trend": "up",
      "trendValue": "+15%",
      "sparkline": [100, 110, 105, 120, 140, 160, 180]
    },
    {
      "id": "users",
      "type": "metric_card",
      "regionId": "kpi-row",
      "title": "Active Users",
      "value": "45K",
      "status": "good",
      "trend": "up",
      "trendValue": "+8%"
    },
    {
      "id": "churn",
      "type": "metric_card",
      "regionId": "kpi-row",
      "title": "Churn Rate",
      "value": "2.1%",
      "status": "warning",
      "trend": "up",
      "trendValue": "+0.3%",
      "target": "< 2%"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Status Indicators
docs.text:
Metric cards support status colors:

Status Use Case good On target, positive trend warning Needs attention critical Requires action neutral Informational

docs.title: Charts in Dashboards
docs.text:
Add charts to the main content area:

{
  "id": "revenue-trend",
  "type": "chart",
  "regionId": "main",
  "title": "Revenue Trend",
  "chartType": "line",
  "data": {
    "labels": ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun"],
    "series": [
      {"name": "2024", "values": [100, 120, 115, 140, 155, 170]},
      {"name": "2025", "values": [110, 135, 145, 165, 180, 200]}
    ]
  },
  "options": {
    "showLegend": true,
    "showGrid": true,
    "yAxisLabel": "Revenue ($K)"
  }
}

docs.title: Metric Dashboard Layout
docs.text:
For all-metrics slides, use metric_dashboard:

{
  "layout": {
    "template": "metric_dashboard"
  },
  "widgets": [
    {"id": "m1", "type": "metric_card", "title": "Revenue", ...},
    {"id": "m2", "type": "metric_card", "title": "Users", ...},
    {"id": "m3", "type": "metric_card", "title": "NPS", ...},
    {"id": "m4", "type": "metric_card", "title": "Churn", ...},
    {"id": "m5", "type": "metric_card", "title": "CAC", ...},
    {"id": "m6", "type": "metric_card", "title": "LTV", ...}
  ]
}

This auto-fits metrics into a grid.

docs.title: Live Data
docs.text:
Connect dashboards to live data sources:

{
  "dataSources": [
    {
      "id": "live-metrics",
      "type": "api",
      "endpoint": "https://api.example.com/metrics",
      "auth": {"type": "bearer", "token": "${API_TOKEN}"},
      "refresh": "30s"
    }
  ],
  "slides": [
    {
      "widgets": [
        {
          "id": "revenue",
          "type": "metric_card",
          "dataSourceId": "live-metrics",
          "dataPath": "revenue"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "metadata": {
    "title": "Q1 Dashboard",
    "author": "Analytics Team"
  },
  "theme": {
    "name": "corporate",
    "aspectRatio": "16:9"
  },
  "slides": [
    {
      "id": "dashboard",
      "type": "dashboard",
      "title": "Q1 2025 Performance",
      "keyMessage": "All metrics trending positive",
      "layout": {
        "template": "kpi_row_content_grid"
      },
      "widgets": [
        {
          "id": "revenue",
          "type": "metric_card",
          "regionId": "kpi-row",
          "title": "Revenue",
          "value": "1.2M",
          "unit": "$",
          "status": "good",
          "trend": "up",
          "trendValue": "+15%"
        },
        {
          "id": "users",
          "type": "metric_card",
          "regionId": "kpi-row",
          "title": "Users",
          "value": "45K",
          "status": "good",
          "trend": "up",
          "trendValue": "+8%"
        },
        {
          "id": "chart",
          "type": "chart",
          "regionId": "main",
          "title": "Monthly Trend",
          "chartType": "line",
          "data": {
            "labels": ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar"],
            "series": [
              {"name": "Revenue", "values": [100, 120, 145]}
            ]
          }
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Working with Data Sources
docs.text: This guide covers connecting presentations to external data.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Data sources allow widgets to pull data from files or APIs instead of hardcoding values. This enables:

  • Dynamic dashboards - Live metrics from APIs
  • Reusable templates - Same presentation, different data
  • Data separation - Keep data in JSON/CSV files

docs.title: JSON Files
docs.text:
Create a data file:

// data/metrics.json
{
  "revenue": {
    "value": "1.2M",
    "trend": "+15%",
    "status": "good"
  },
  "users": {
    "value": "45K",
    "trend": "+8%",
    "status": "good"
  }
}

Reference it in your presentation:

{
  "dataSources": [
    {
      "id": "metrics",
      "type": "json",
      "path": "./data/metrics.json"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: CSV Files
docs.text:
For tabular data, use CSV:

month,revenue,users
Jan,100000,40000
Feb,120000,42000
Mar,145000,45000

{
  "dataSources": [
    {
      "id": "monthly-data",
      "type": "csv",
      "path": "./data/monthly.csv"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Basic API
docs.text:
{
  "dataSources": [
    {
      "id": "api-metrics",
      "type": "api",
      "endpoint": "https://api.example.com/metrics"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: With Authentication
docs.text:
Bearer Token:

{
  "id": "secure-api",
  "type": "api",
  "endpoint": "https://api.example.com/data",
  "auth": {
    "type": "bearer",
    "token": "${API_TOKEN}"
  }
}

Basic Auth:

{
  "auth": {
    "type": "basic",
    "username": "${API_USER}",
    "password": "${API_PASS}"
  }
}

API Key:

{
  "auth": {
    "type": "apikey",
    "header": "X-API-Key",
    "key": "${API_KEY}"
  }
}

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Use ${VAR_NAME} to reference environment variables:

{
  "endpoint": "${API_BASE_URL}/metrics",
  "auth": {
    "type": "bearer",
    "token": "${API_TOKEN}"
  }
}

Set variables before rendering:

export API_BASE_URL="https://api.example.com"
export API_TOKEN="your-token"
presspec render presentation.json --output ./output

docs.title: dataSourceId
docs.text:
Reference the data source:

{
  "id": "revenue-card",
  "type": "metric_card",
  "dataSourceId": "metrics"
}

docs.title: dataPath
docs.text:
Extract specific data using JSON path:

{
  "id": "revenue-card",
  "type": "metric_card",
  "dataSourceId": "metrics",
  "dataPath": "revenue"
}

For nested data:

{
  "dataPath": "quarterly.q1.revenue"
}

docs.title: Chart Data Binding
docs.text:
Bind chart series to data:

{
  "type": "chart",
  "chartType": "line",
  "dataSourceId": "monthly-data",
  "data": {
    "labelsPath": "months",
    "seriesPath": "values"
  }
}

docs.title: Live Refresh
docs.text:
For real-time dashboards, set refresh intervals:

{
  "id": "live-kpis",
  "type": "api",
  "endpoint": "https://api.example.com/live",
  "refresh": "30s"
}

Supported intervals:

Format Example Description Ns 30s N seconds Nm 5m N minutes Nh 1h N hours

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
When data loading fails:

  1. File not found - Check path is relative to presentation file
  2. API error - Verify endpoint and credentials
  3. Parse error - Validate JSON/CSV format

The CLI shows detailed errors:

presspec render presentation.json -v
# Error: failed to load data source "metrics": file not found: ./data/metrics.json

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Use environment variables for secrets - Never hardcode API tokens
  2. Keep data files alongside presentation - Use relative paths
  3. Validate data format - Ensure data matches widget expectations
  4. Set appropriate refresh intervals - Balance freshness vs. API load

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "metadata": {
    "title": "Live Dashboard"
  },
  "dataSources": [
    {
      "id": "local-config",
      "type": "json",
      "path": "./data/config.json"
    },
    {
      "id": "live-metrics",
      "type": "api",
      "endpoint": "${API_URL}/metrics",
      "auth": {
        "type": "bearer",
        "token": "${API_TOKEN}"
      },
      "refresh": "1m"
    }
  ],
  "slides": [
    {
      "id": "dashboard",
      "type": "dashboard",
      "widgets": [
        {
          "id": "revenue",
          "type": "metric_card",
          "title": "Revenue",
          "dataSourceId": "live-metrics",
          "dataPath": "kpis.revenue"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Speaker Notes
docs.text: This guide covers using speaker notes in presentations.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Speaker notes provide presenter-only content that doesn't appear on slides. PresentationSpec supports:

  • Per-slide speaker notes
  • Dedicated speaker view with timer
  • Keyboard navigation

docs.title: Adding Speaker Notes
docs.text:
Add notes to any slide:

{
  "id": "overview",
  "type": "content",
  "title": "Product Overview",
  "speakerNotes": "Start with the problem statement. Mention customer feedback from Q4 survey.",
  "widgets": [...]
}

docs.title: Markdown Support
docs.text:
Speaker notes support markdown formatting:

{
  "speakerNotes": "## Key Points

- Revenue up 15%
- User growth exceeds target
- **Emphasize** the Q2 launch date"
}

docs.title: Generating Speaker View
docs.text:
Generate a separate speaker notes HTML file:

presspec render presentation.json --output ./output --speaker-notes

This creates:

output/
├── index.html          # Main presentation
├── speaker-notes.html  # Speaker view
└── assets/

docs.title: Speaker View Features
docs.text: The speaker view includes:

docs.title: Timer
docs.text:
• Elapsed time display
  • Start/pause/reset controls

docs.title: Slide Preview
docs.text:
• Current slide thumbnail
  • Next slide preview

docs.title: Notes Panel
docs.text:
• Current slide notes (rendered markdown)
  • Large, readable text

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text: Key Action → / Space Next slide ← Previous slide Home First slide End Last slide

docs.title: Dual-Screen Setup
docs.text:
For presentations:

  1. Open index.html on the projector/external display
  2. Open speaker-notes.html on your laptop screen
  3. Navigate from either window (they stay in sync via localStorage)

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "metadata": {
    "title": "Q1 Review"
  },
  "slides": [
    {
      "id": "title",
      "type": "title",
      "title": "Q1 2025 Review",
      "speakerNotes": "Welcome everyone. This is our quarterly review covering January through March."
    },
    {
      "id": "agenda",
      "type": "content",
      "title": "Agenda",
      "speakerNotes": "## Topics\n\n1. Financial highlights (5 min)\n2. Product updates (10 min)\n3. Q2 roadmap (5 min)\n4. Q&A (10 min)\n\n**Total: 30 minutes**",
      "widgets": [...]
    },
    {
      "id": "revenue",
      "type": "dashboard",
      "title": "Revenue",
      "speakerNotes": "Key message: Revenue up 15% YoY.\n\n- Driven by enterprise segment\n- APAC grew 25%\n- Pause for questions if needed"
    },
    {
      "id": "summary",
      "type": "summary",
      "title": "Summary",
      "speakerNotes": "Recap the three key points:\n\n1. Strong financial performance\n2. Product momentum\n3. Clear Q2 priorities\n\nThank the team for their contributions."
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Keep notes concise - Bullet points over paragraphs
  2. Include timing cues - Note when to pause or ask questions
  3. Add data reminders - Key numbers you want to emphasize
  4. Use markdown headers - Organize longer notes
  5. Practice with speaker view - Rehearse with the timer running

docs.title: Customizing Themes
docs.text: This guide covers creating and customizing presentation themes.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Themes control the visual appearance of presentations:

  • Colors
  • Typography
  • Branding
  • Layout density
  • Aspect ratio

docs.title: Basic Theme
docs.text:
{
  "theme": {
    "name": "my-theme",
    "aspectRatio": "16:9"
  }
}

docs.title: Color Palette
docs.text:
{
  "theme": {
    "colors": {
      "primary": "#1a73e8",
      "secondary": "#34a853",
      "accent": "#ea4335",
      "background": "#ffffff",
      "surface": "#f8f9fa",
      "text": "#1e293b",
      "textMuted": "#64748b"
    }
  }
}
Color Usage primary Main brand color, buttons, links secondary Secondary actions, accents accent Highlights, call-to-action background Page background surface Card and widget backgrounds text Primary text textMuted Secondary text, captions

docs.title: Status Colors
docs.text:
{
  "colors": {
    "success": "#22c55e",
    "warning": "#f59e0b",
    "error": "#ef4444"
  }
}

These are used by metric cards and status indicators.

docs.title: Typography
docs.text:
{
  "theme": {
    "typography": {
      "fontFamily": "Inter, system-ui, sans-serif",
      "headingFontFamily": "Inter, system-ui, sans-serif",
      "codeFontFamily": "JetBrains Mono, monospace",
      "baseFontSize": "16px",
      "lineHeight": "1.6"
    }
  }
}
Property Description fontFamily Body text font stack headingFontFamily Heading font stack codeFontFamily Code block font baseFontSize Base font size lineHeight Base line height

docs.title: Branding
docs.text:
Add company branding:

{
  "theme": {
    "brand": {
      "logo": "/assets/logo.png",
      "logoPosition": "top-right",
      "companyName": "Acme Corp",
      "primaryColor": "#1a73e8",
      "secondaryColor": "#34a853"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Logo Positions
docs.text: Position Description top-left Upper left corner top-right Upper right corner bottom-left Lower left corner bottom-right Lower right corner

docs.title: Density
docs.text:
Control spacing and content density:

{
  "theme": {
    "density": "normal"
  }
}
Density Use Case compact More content, tighter spacing normal Balanced (default) spacious More whitespace, fewer elements

docs.title: Aspect Ratio
docs.text:
{
  "theme": {
    "aspectRatio": "16:9"
  }
}
Ratio Use Case 16:9 Widescreen displays (default) 4:3 Traditional screens 1:1 Square format

docs.title: Dark Mode Theme
docs.text:
{
  "theme": {
    "name": "dark-mode",
    "colors": {
      "primary": "#60a5fa",
      "secondary": "#4ade80",
      "accent": "#f472b6",
      "background": "#0f172a",
      "surface": "#1e293b",
      "text": "#f1f5f9",
      "textMuted": "#94a3b8",
      "success": "#22c55e",
      "warning": "#fbbf24",
      "error": "#f87171"
    },
    "typography": {
      "fontFamily": "Inter, sans-serif",
      "codeFontFamily": "Fira Code, monospace"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Corporate Theme
docs.text:
{
  "theme": {
    "name": "corporate",
    "density": "normal",
    "aspectRatio": "16:9",
    "brand": {
      "logo": "/assets/company-logo.png",
      "logoPosition": "top-right",
      "companyName": "Acme Corp"
    },
    "colors": {
      "primary": "#0f4c81",
      "secondary": "#2e7d32",
      "accent": "#ff6f00",
      "background": "#ffffff",
      "surface": "#f5f5f5",
      "text": "#212121",
      "textMuted": "#757575"
    },
    "typography": {
      "fontFamily": "Roboto, sans-serif",
      "headingFontFamily": "Roboto Slab, serif",
      "baseFontSize": "18px"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Minimal Theme
docs.text:
{
  "theme": {
    "name": "minimal",
    "density": "spacious",
    "colors": {
      "primary": "#000000",
      "secondary": "#666666",
      "background": "#ffffff",
      "surface": "#fafafa",
      "text": "#1a1a1a",
      "textMuted": "#888888"
    },
    "typography": {
      "fontFamily": "system-ui, sans-serif",
      "baseFontSize": "20px",
      "lineHeight": "1.8"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Theme Inheritance
docs.text:
Override specific properties while keeping defaults:

{
  "theme": {
    "name": "custom",
    "colors": {
      "primary": "#your-brand-color"
    }
  }
}

Only specified properties are overridden.

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Use consistent colors - Match your brand guidelines
  2. Test readability - Ensure sufficient contrast
  3. Consider accessibility - Use WCAG-compliant color combinations
  4. Use web-safe fonts - Or include font files
  5. Test on target displays - Check colors on projectors/screens

docs.title: Release Notes: v0.1.0
docs.text:
Release Date: 2025-01-15

This is the initial release of PresentationSpec, a Go-first specification library for creating data-driven presentations.

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• Go-first design: Go types are the source of truth, JSON Schema is generated automatically
  • Multiple output formats: HTML and PDF export out of the box
  • Rich widget library: 12 widget types for dashboards and executive presentations
  • Live development: Dev server with hot reload for rapid iteration
  • Data integration: Load data from files and APIs directly into presentations

docs.title: Core Specification
docs.text:
• PresentationSpec type with metadata, theme, narrative, slides, data sources, and export configuration
  • 12 widget types:
  • metric_card - KPI metrics with trends and sparklines
  • content_block - Markdown/HTML content
  • chart - Bar, line, pie, area, and more via Chart.js
  • table - Data tables with configurable columns
  • risk_list - Risk items with severity and mitigation
  • decision_list - Decisions requiring action
  • checklist - Task lists with completion status
  • diagram - Mermaid, PlantUML, Graphviz, D2 diagrams
  • callout - Info, warning, error, success, tip callouts
  • quote - Quotations with attribution
  • image - Images with captions

  • code - Code blocks with syntax highlighting

  • 6 slide types: title, content, dashboard, section, summary, blank

  • Layout system with predefined templates and custom regions
  • Theme support with colors, typography, density, and aspect ratio

docs.title: Schema Generation
docs.text:
• JSON Schema generated from Go types using invopop/jsonschema
  • Embedded schema for runtime validation
  • CLI tool (genschema) for schema regeneration

docs.title: HTML Rendering
docs.text:
• Static HTML output via a-h/templ templates
  • Responsive CSS with CSS custom properties
  • Keyboard navigation (arrow keys, space, home/end)
  • Hash-based slide linking

docs.title: PDF Export
docs.text:
• PDF generation via headless Chrome (chromedp)
  • Configurable page size (Letter, A4, Legal, Tabloid)
  • Landscape/portrait orientation
  • Background graphics support

docs.title: Development Server
docs.text:
• Live reload on file changes
  • Automatic rebuild with error reporting
  • Configurable port and host
  • render serve command

docs.title: Watch Mode
docs.text:
• File watching with configurable interval
  • Auto-rebuild on spec changes
  • render --watch flag

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text:
• JSON file loading - Load data from local JSON files
  • CSV file loading - Parse CSV with headers as keys
  • REST API loading - Fetch data from HTTP endpoints
  • Authentication support:
  • Bearer token
  • Basic auth
  • API key (custom header)

docs.title: Syntax Highlighting
docs.text:
• Prism.js integration via CDN
  • Auto-detection of language from language attribute
  • Tomorrow theme (dark)

docs.title: Diagram Support
docs.text:
• Mermaid.js integration via CDN
  • Support for flowcharts, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, etc.

docs.title: Chart Support
docs.text:
• Chart.js integration via CDN
  • Chart types: bar, line, pie, donut, area, scatter, radar
  • Configurable options (legend, grid, axis labels)
  • SVG fallback for basic charts

docs.title: Speaker Notes View
docs.text:
• Separate speaker.html with presenter view
  • Current slide and next slide preview
  • Speaker notes panel
  • Presentation timer (start/pause/reset)
  • Keyboard navigation
  • render --speaker-notes flag

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
• JSON Schema validation via xeipuuv/gojsonschema
  • render --validate flag for pre-render validation
  • render --validate-only flag for validation without rendering

docs.title: CLI Commands
docs.text:
# Render to HTML
render presentation.json -o output/

# Render to PDF
render presentation.json --format pdf -o presentation.pdf

# Validate spec
render presentation.json --validate-only

# Watch mode
render presentation.json -o output/ --watch

# Dev server
render serve presentation.json --port 8080

# Generate speaker notes
render presentation.json -o output/ --speaker-notes

# Generate schema
genschema -o schema/presentation.schema.json

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
presentation-spec/
├── spec/           # Core Go types (source of truth)
├── schema/         # JSON Schema generation and validation
├── templ/          # HTML rendering templates
│   └── widgets/    # Widget templates
├── cmd/
│   ├── render/     # CLI for rendering
│   └── genschema/  # CLI for schema generation
├── examples/       # Example presentations
│   ├── basic/
│   └── dashboard/
└── docs/
    ├── specs/      # Specifications
    └── releases/   # Release notes

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• github.com/a-h/templ - HTML templating
  • github.com/spf13/cobra - CLI framework
  • github.com/invopop/jsonschema - JSON Schema generation
  • github.com/xeipuuv/gojsonschema - JSON Schema validation
  • github.com/chromedp/chromedp - Headless Chrome for PDF

docs.title: Test Coverage
docs.text:
• 33 unit tests across packages
  • Tests for data loading, rendering, validation, and spec types

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
• Database data sources not yet implemented
  • PPTX export not yet available
  • No custom theme file loading (use inline theme in spec)
  • Mermaid/Chart.js require internet connection (CDN)

docs.title: Upgrade Notes
docs.text: This is the initial release. No upgrade path required.

docs.title: Contributors
docs.text: Initial implementation by the PresentationSpec team.

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• Roadmap
  • Specification

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text: Data sources connect presentations to external data for dynamic content.

docs.title: Structure
docs.text:
{
  "dataSources": [
    {
      "id": "metrics",
      "type": "json",
      "path": "./data/metrics.json"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Fields
docs.text: Field Type Required Description id string Yes Unique identifier type string Yes Source type path string No File path (for file sources) endpoint string No URL (for API sources) auth Auth No Authentication config refresh string No Refresh interval

docs.title: JSON File
docs.text:
Load data from a local JSON file.

{
  "id": "metrics",
  "type": "json",
  "path": "./data/metrics.json"
}

docs.title: CSV File
docs.text:
Load tabular data from CSV.

{
  "id": "sales",
  "type": "csv",
  "path": "./data/sales.csv"
}

docs.title: REST API
docs.text:
Fetch data from an HTTP endpoint.

{
  "id": "live-metrics",
  "type": "api",
  "endpoint": "https://api.example.com/metrics",
  "auth": {
    "type": "bearer",
    "token": "${API_TOKEN}"
  }
}

docs.title: Bearer Token
docs.text:
{
  "auth": {
    "type": "bearer",
    "token": "${API_TOKEN}"
  }
}

docs.title: Basic Auth
docs.text:
{
  "auth": {
    "type": "basic",
    "username": "${API_USER}",
    "password": "${API_PASS}"
  }
}

docs.title: API Key
docs.text:
{
  "auth": {
    "type": "apikey",
    "header": "X-API-Key",
    "key": "${API_KEY}"
  }
}

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Use ${VAR_NAME} syntax to reference environment variables:

{
  "endpoint": "${API_BASE_URL}/metrics",
  "auth": {
    "type": "bearer",
    "token": "${API_TOKEN}"
  }
}

docs.title: Widget Binding
docs.text:
Widgets reference data sources via dataSourceId:

{
  "widgets": [
    {
      "id": "revenue-chart",
      "type": "chart",
      "dataSourceId": "metrics",
      "dataPath": "revenue.monthly"
    }
  ]
}
Field Description dataSourceId Reference to data source dataPath JSON path to extract data

docs.title: Refresh Intervals
docs.text:
For live data, specify refresh intervals:

{
  "id": "realtime",
  "type": "api",
  "endpoint": "https://api.example.com/live",
  "refresh": "30s"
}
Value Description 30s Every 30 seconds 5m Every 5 minutes 1h Every hour

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
Complete data source configuration:

{
  "dataSources": [
    {
      "id": "local-metrics",
      "type": "json",
      "path": "./data/q1-metrics.json"
    },
    {
      "id": "sales-data",
      "type": "csv",
      "path": "./data/sales-2025.csv"
    },
    {
      "id": "live-kpis",
      "type": "api",
      "endpoint": "${API_URL}/kpis",
      "auth": {
        "type": "bearer",
        "token": "${API_TOKEN}"
      },
      "refresh": "5m"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Layouts
docs.text: Layouts define how widgets are arranged on a slide.

docs.title: Structure
docs.text:
{
  "layout": {

Widgets reference regions via regionId:

docs.title: Specification Overview
docs.text: PresentationSpec is a format-agnostic intermediate representation (IR) for presentations. It defines the structure and content of presentations in a way that can be rendered to multiple output formats.

docs.title: Go-First
docs.text:
Go types are the source of truth. The JSON Schema is generated from Go structs, not hand-written. This ensures:

  • Type safety in Go code
  • Automatic schema updates when types change
  • Consistent validation across languages

docs.title: Format-Agnostic
docs.text:
The spec describes what to present, not how to render it. This enables:

  • Multiple output formats (HTML, PDF, PPTX)
  • Different themes and layouts
  • Future format support without spec changes

docs.title: Data-Driven
docs.text:
Presentations can reference external data sources, enabling:

  • Real-time data in presentations
  • Automated dashboard generation
  • Data refresh without spec changes

docs.title: Structure
docs.text:
A PresentationSpec consists of:

PresentationSpec
├── version          # Spec version
├── metadata         # Title, author, date, tags
├── theme            # Visual styling
├── narrative        # Story structure
├── slides[]         # Content slides
│   ├── layout       # Slide layout
│   └── widgets[]    # Content elements
├── dataSources[]    # External data
└── exports          # Export configuration

docs.title: Core Types
docs.text: Type Description PresentationSpec Root container for a presentation Metadata Title, description, author, date Theme Colors, typography, density Narrative Story structure with sections SlideSpec Individual slide content Widget Content element (metric, chart, etc.) Layout Slide layout template DataSource External data reference ExportConfig Export format settings

docs.title: Slide Types
docs.text: Type Use Case title Opening slide with title and subtitle content Standard content slide dashboard Data-heavy KPI dashboard section Section divider summary Conclusion or summary blank Empty slide for custom content

docs.title: Widget Types
docs.text: Type Use Case metric_card Single KPI with trend chart Data visualization table Tabular data risk_list Risk items with severity decision_list Decisions needing action checklist Task completion list content_block Markdown/HTML text diagram Mermaid/PlantUML diagrams callout Highlighted information quote Quotations image Images with captions code Code with syntax highlighting

docs.title: JSON Schema
docs.text:
The JSON Schema is available at:

schema/presentation.schema.json

Use it for:

  • Editor autocompletion (VS Code, JetBrains)
  • Validation in CI/CD pipelines
  • Documentation generation

docs.title: VS Code Setup
docs.text:
Add to your .vscode/settings.json:

{
  "json.schemas": [
    {
      "fileMatch": ["*.presentation.json"],
      "url": "./schema/presentation.schema.json"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Versioning
docs.text:
The spec uses semantic versioning:

  • Major: Breaking changes to spec structure
  • Minor: New fields or widget types (backwards compatible)
  • Patch: Documentation or schema fixes

Current version: 1.0

docs.title: Presentation
docs.text: The root PresentationSpec type defines a complete presentation.

docs.title: Structure
docs.text:
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "metadata": { ... },
  "theme": { ... },
  "narrative": { ... },
  "slides": [ ... ],
  "dataSources": [ ... ],
  "exports": { ... }
}

docs.title: Fields
docs.text: Field Type Required Description version string Yes Spec version (e.g., "1.0") metadata Metadata Yes Presentation metadata theme Theme No Visual styling narrative Narrative No Story structure slides SlideSpec[] Yes Presentation slides dataSources DataSource[] No External data sources exports ExportConfig No Export configuration

docs.title: Metadata
docs.text:
{
  "metadata": {
    "title": "Q1 2025 Review",
    "description": "Quarterly business review",
    "author": "Product Team",
    "date": "2025-01-15",
    "tags": ["quarterly", "review", "2025"]
  }
}
Field Type Description title string Presentation title description string Brief description author string Author name date string Creation date (ISO 8601) tags string[] Categorization tags

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "metadata": {
    "title": "Product Launch",
    "author": "Launch Team",
    "date": "2025-01-15"
  },
  "theme": {
    "name": "corporate",
    "aspectRatio": "16:9"
  },
  "slides": [
    {
      "id": "title",
      "type": "title",
      "title": "Product Launch 2025"
    },
    {
      "id": "agenda",
      "type": "content",
      "title": "Agenda",
      "widgets": [
        {
          "id": "agenda-list",
          "type": "content_block",
          "content": "1. Overview\n2. Timeline\n3. Resources\n4. Q&A"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Slides
docs.text: Slides are the main content containers in a presentation.

docs.title: Structure
docs.text:
{
  "id": "dashboard",
  "sectionId": "metrics",
  "type": "dashboard",
  "title": "Key Metrics",
  "keyMessage": "All metrics trending positive",
  "layout": { ... },
  "widgets": [ ... ],
  "speakerNotes": "Emphasize Q1 growth",
  "quality": { ... }
}

docs.title: Fields
docs.text: Field Type Required Description id string Yes Unique slide identifier sectionId string No Link to narrative section type SlideType Yes Slide type title string No Slide title keyMessage string No Main takeaway layout Layout No Layout configuration widgets Widget[] No Content widgets speakerNotes string No Presenter notes quality Quality No Quality metadata

docs.title: Title
docs.text:
Opening slide with prominent title.

{
  "id": "title",
  "type": "title",
  "title": "Q1 2025 Results",
  "widgets": [
    {
      "id": "subtitle",
      "type": "content_block",
      "content": "Executive Review | January 2025"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Content
docs.text:
Standard content slide.

{
  "id": "overview",
  "type": "content",
  "title": "Overview",
  "keyMessage": "Strong performance across all metrics",
  "widgets": [ ... ]
}

docs.title: Dashboard
docs.text:
Data-heavy slide with multiple metrics.

{
  "id": "kpis",
  "type": "dashboard",
  "title": "KPI Dashboard",
  "layout": {
    "template": "kpi_row_content_grid"
  },
  "widgets": [
    {"type": "metric_card", ...},
    {"type": "chart", ...}
  ]
}

docs.title: Section
docs.text:
Section divider slide.

{
  "id": "section-1",
  "type": "section",
  "title": "Financial Results"
}

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Conclusion slide.

{
  "id": "summary",
  "type": "summary",
  "title": "Key Takeaways",
  "keyMessage": "On track for Q2 launch"
}

docs.title: Blank
docs.text:
Empty slide for custom content.

{
  "id": "custom",
  "type": "blank",
  "widgets": [ ... ]
}

docs.title: Quality Metadata
docs.text:
Track data freshness and confidence.

{
  "quality": {
    "dataFreshness": "real-time",
    "confidence": 0.95,
    "sources": ["analytics", "crm"],
    "lastUpdated": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"
  }
}
Field Type Description dataFreshness string Data recency description confidence number Confidence level (0.0-1.0) sources string[] Data source names lastUpdated string Last update timestamp

docs.title: Themes
docs.text: Themes control the visual styling of presentations.

docs.title: Structure
docs.text:
{
  "theme": {
    "name": "corporate",
    "density": "normal",
    "aspectRatio": "16:9",
    "brand": { ... },
    "colors": { ... },
    "typography": { ... }
  }
}

docs.title: Fields
docs.text: Field Type Description name string Theme name density string compact, normal, spacious aspectRatio string 16:9, 4:3, 1:1 brand Brand Branding options colors ColorPalette Color scheme typography Typography Font settings

docs.title: Density
docs.text: Value Description compact Tighter spacing, more content normal Balanced spacing (default) spacious More whitespace

docs.title: Aspect Ratio
docs.text: Value Use Case 16:9 Widescreen (default) 4:3 Traditional screens 1:1 Square format

docs.title: Brand
docs.text:
{
  "brand": {
    "logo": "/assets/logo.png",
    "logoPosition": "top-right",
    "companyName": "Acme Corp",
    "primaryColor": "#1a73e8",
    "secondaryColor": "#34a853"
  }
}
Field Type Description logo string Logo image path logoPosition string top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right companyName string Company name primaryColor string Primary brand color (hex) secondaryColor string Secondary brand color (hex)

docs.title: Colors
docs.text:
{
  "colors": {
    "primary": "#1a73e8",

docs.text:
{
  "typography": {
    "fontFamily": "Inter, system-ui, sans-serif",

    "name": "dark-mode",

docs.title: Widgets
docs.text: Widgets are the content elements within slides. Each widget has a type field that determines its structure and rendering.

docs.title: Common Fields
docs.text:
All widgets share these fields:

Field Type Description id string Unique identifier type string Widget type discriminator title string Widget title regionId string Target layout region dataSourceId string Reference to data source

docs.title: Metric Card
docs.text:
Display a single KPI metric with optional trend and sparkline.

{
  "id": "revenue",
  "type": "metric_card",
  "title": "Revenue",
  "value": "1.2M",
  "unit": "$",
  "status": "good",
  "trend": "up",
  "trendValue": "+15%",
  "target": "1M",
  "sparkline": [100, 120, 115, 140, 160, 180]
}
Field Type Description value string Primary metric value unit string Unit of measurement status string good, warning, critical, neutral trend string up, down, flat trendValue string Trend amount (e.g., "+15%") target string Target value sparkline number[] Data points for sparkline

docs.title: Chart
docs.text:
Data visualization using Chart.js.

{
  "id": "sales-chart",
  "type": "chart",
  "title": "Sales Trend",
  "chartType": "line",
  "data": {
    "labels": ["Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4"],
    "series": [
      {"name": "2024", "values": [100, 150, 200, 250]},
      {"name": "2025", "values": [120, 180, 220, 300]}
    ]
  },
  "options": {
    "showLegend": true,
    "showGrid": true,
    "yAxisLabel": "Revenue ($K)"
  }
}
Field Type Description chartType string bar, line, pie, donut, area, scatter, radar data.labels string[] X-axis labels data.series array Data series with name, values, color options.showLegend boolean Show legend options.showGrid boolean Show grid lines options.stacked boolean Stack series options.xAxisLabel string X-axis label options.yAxisLabel string Y-axis label

docs.title: Table
docs.text:
Tabular data display.

{
  "id": "features-table",
  "type": "table",
  "title": "Feature Status",
  "columns": [
    {"key": "feature", "header": "Feature", "width": "50%"},
    {"key": "owner", "header": "Owner", "width": "25%"},
    {"key": "status", "header": "Status", "width": "25%", "align": "center"}
  ],
  "rows": [
    {"feature": "User Auth", "owner": "Alice", "status": "Complete"},
    {"feature": "Dashboard", "owner": "Bob", "status": "In Progress"}
  ]
}
Field Type Description columns array Column definitions columns[].key string Row data key columns[].header string Column header text columns[].width string Column width columns[].align string left, center, right rows array Row data objects

docs.title: Risk List
docs.text:
Display risks with severity and mitigation.

{
  "id": "risks",
  "type": "risk_list",
  "title": "Open Risks",
  "items": [
    {
      "text": "API rate limits may impact launch",
      "severity": "high",
      "mitigation": "Implement caching layer",
      "owner": "Platform Team"
    },
    {
      "text": "Documentation incomplete",
      "severity": "medium",
      "mitigation": "Doc sprint this week",
      "owner": "Tech Writing"
    }
  ]
}
Field Type Description items array Risk items items[].text string Risk description items[].severity string low, medium, high, critical items[].mitigation string Mitigation plan items[].owner string Responsible party items[].status string Item status

docs.title: Decision List
docs.text:
Display decisions requiring action.

{
  "id": "decisions",
  "type": "decision_list",
  "title": "Pending Decisions",
  "items": [
    {
      "text": "Choose deployment strategy",
      "description": "Phased rollout vs big-bang launch",
      "owner": "VP Product",
      "deadline": "2025-01-20"
    }
  ]
}
Field Type Description items array Decision items items[].text string Decision title items[].description string Additional context items[].owner string Decision maker items[].deadline string Due date

docs.title: Checklist
docs.text:
Task completion list.

{
  "id": "launch-checklist",
  "type": "checklist",
  "title": "Launch Checklist",
  "items": [
    {"text": "Feature freeze", "checked": true},
    {"text": "Security review", "checked": true},
    {"text": "Load testing", "checked": false},
    {"text": "Documentation", "checked": false}
  ]
}
Field Type Description items array Checklist items items[].text string Item text items[].checked boolean Completion status

docs.title: Content Block
docs.text:
Formatted text content.

{
  "id": "summary",
  "type": "content_block",
  "title": "Summary",
  "content": "## Key Points\n\n- Revenue up 15%\n- User growth on track\n- Launch scheduled for Q2",
  "format": "markdown"
}
Field Type Description content string Text content format string markdown, html, plain

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
Diagrams via Mermaid or other tools.

{
  "id": "architecture",
  "type": "diagram",
  "title": "System Architecture",
  "diagramType": "mermaid",
  "source": "graph LR\n  A[Client] --> B[API]\n  B --> C[Database]"
}
Field Type Description diagramType string mermaid, plantuml, graphviz, d2 source string Diagram source code

docs.title: Callout
docs.text:
Highlighted information box.

{
  "id": "warning",
  "type": "callout",
  "title": "Important",
  "calloutType": "warning",
  "content": "API changes require client updates before launch."
}
Field Type Description calloutType string info, warning, error, success, tip content string Callout text

docs.title: Quote
docs.text:
Quotation with attribution.

{
  "id": "quote",
  "type": "quote",
  "quote": "The best way to predict the future is to create it.",
  "attribution": "Peter Drucker"
}
Field Type Description quote string Quoted text attribution string Quote source

docs.title: Image
docs.text:
Image with optional caption.

{
  "id": "hero",
  "type": "image",
  "src": "/images/hero.png",
  "alt": "Product screenshot",
  "caption": "New dashboard design"
}
Field Type Description src string Image URL or path alt string Alt text for accessibility caption string Image caption

docs.title: Code
docs.text:
Code block with syntax highlighting.

{
  "id": "example",
  "type": "code",
  "title": "API Example",
  "language": "go",
  "code": "func main() {\n  fmt.Println(\"Hello\")\n}",
  "highlight": [2]
}
Field Type Description language string Programming language code string Code content highlight number[] Lines to highlight

docs.title: PresentationSpec Roadmap
docs.text: This document tracks planned features and enhancements for PresentationSpec.

docs.title: Completed Features
docs.text: Feature Description Status Core spec types Go-first type definitions for presentations ✓ Done JSON Schema generation Auto-generate schema from Go types ✓ Done HTML rendering Static HTML output via templ templates ✓ Done PDF export PDF generation via headless Chrome (chromedp) ✓ Done Schema validation Validate specs against JSON Schema ✓ Done Watch mode Auto-rebuild on file changes ✓ Done Dev server Live reload development server ✓ Done Data source loading Load data from JSON, CSV, REST APIs ✓ Done Syntax highlighting Code blocks via Prism.js ✓ Done Mermaid diagrams Diagram rendering via Mermaid.js ✓ Done Chart.js integration Rich charts via Chart.js ✓ Done Speaker notes view Presenter view with timer and notes ✓ Done Unit tests Test coverage for core packages ✓ Done

docs.title: PPTX Export
docs.text:
• Description: Export presentations to PowerPoint format
  • Implementation: Use github.com/unidoc/unioffice library
  • Complexity: High
  • Notes: Map widgets to PowerPoint shapes, handle layouts

docs.title: Image Export
docs.text:
• Description: Export individual slides as PNG or SVG images
  • Implementation: Use chromedp to capture screenshots
  • Complexity: Medium
  • Notes: Useful for sharing slides on social media, embedding in docs

docs.title: Custom Theme System
docs.text:
• Description: Allow user-defined themes via JSON/YAML configuration

  • Complexity: Medium

docs.title: Reveal.js Export
docs.text:
• Description: Export to Reveal.js HTML presentation format
